steps.title: Identify favorable and total outcomes
steps.content:
In a probability problem, we count favorable outcomes (what we want) and total outcomes (all possibilities). Here:
  • Total houses on the street: 7
  • Blue houses (favorable): 2

steps.title: Apply the probability formula
steps.content:
The probability of selecting a blue house is the ratio of blue houses to total houses:
$$P(\text{blue house}) = \frac{\text{blue houses}}{\text{total houses}} = \frac{2}{7}$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
